Issue: Incorrect minus sign in `rotate_range` parameter documentation for spatial transforms
Decision: Removed incorrect negation in `uniform[f[0], f[1])` documentation for pair case in rotate_range
Rationale: The incorrect negation was removed because the actual implementation uses `uniform(f[0], f[1])` without negation for the pair case, as shown in `_get_rand_param` (line 1908). The single value case correctly uses `uniform(-f, f)` for negation.
Author: yunhaoli24
Description: Spatial transforms (RandAffineGrid, RandAffine, Rand2DElastic, Rand3DElastic, RandAffined, Rand2DElasticd, Rand3DElasticd) in MONAI library
Time stamp: 2026-03-12T06:27:03Z (created), 2026-03-12T06:27:46Z (comment by coderabbitai[bot]), 2026-03-18T18:30:27Z (comment by ericspod), 2026-03-19T12:33:58Z (comment by yunhaoli24)
Cost: Low (documentation update only, no code logic changes)

Argument.author: ericspod
Argument.argument: The line `If not, uniform[-rotate_range[i], rotate_range[i])` should be checked for incorrect negation similar to the first line.

Argument.author: yunhaoli24
Argument.argument: The negative sign in the single value case (`uniform(-f, f)`) is correct as per implementation in `_get_rand_param` (line 1908), while the pair case (`uniform(f[0], f[1])`) correctly has no negation.

Justification: The ticket involves correcting documentation for spatial transforms parameters (rotate_range) in the MONAI library, which directly impacts data augmentation and preprocessing steps in the data pipeline.
